title: Speculaas
subtitle: Dutch spiced biscuits rolled thin and crisp
excerpt: Dutch spiced biscuits flavoured with cinnamon, ginger, cardamom, nutmeg, and clove. Rolled thin and cut into shapes, then baked until crisp and fragrant. The spice blend is the point. Makes about thirty biscuits.
sourceNotes: Speculaas are a Dutch-Flemish tradition documented in the Low Countries from at least the seventeenth century, associated with the feast of Sinterklaas in early December. The spice mix and the thin, crisp construction are documented in Dutch household manuals of the nineteenth century. The form recorded here follows the kruidnagel-cinnamon-ginger-kardamom-nootmuskaat blend noted in Dutch baking literature, without almond flour — the plainer weekday form rather than the richer gevulde (filled) variety.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze baked and fully cooled in an airtight container. Thaw at room temperature.
recipe.batchNotes: Doubles cleanly. Roll in two batches.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The dough is better chilled overnight. The spice flavour deepens as it rests.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; 180°C in a conventional oven

glossaryTerms.definition: The spice blend that defines Dutch speculaas: cinnamon, ginger, cardamom, nutmeg, clove, and sometimes white pepper and anise. The proportions vary by household and region, but cinnamon is always dominant. Pre-mixed versions labelled speculaas spice or mixed spice are available in Dutch and some specialist supermarkets. Use ground spices from fresh jars for the best result; the blend fades quickly once opened.

content.text: The dough for speculaas is a spiced shortbread: butter, sugar, flour, and a mixture of warm spices. The key is the spice ratio. Cinnamon dominates; ginger and cardamom support it; nutmeg and clove are present in the background. Roll the dough as thin as possible — 3 to 4 mm is right.

is what distinguishes this from an ordinary ginger biscuit. Make a large batch of the spice mix and store it in a jar — it keeps for six months and the dough comes together in minutes once it is ready.

Combine the {{plain-flour}}, {{dark-brown-sugar}}, {{caster-sugar}}, {{bicarbonate-of-soda}}, {{cinnamon-ground}}, {{ginger-ground}}, {{cardamom-green}}, {{nutmeg-ground}}, {{cloves}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}} in a large bowl. Rub in the cold {{unsalted-butter}} until the mixture looks like damp sand. Add the {{whole-milk}} and press together until a smooth dough forms. Wrap and chill for at least 1 hour, or overnight.

Preheat the oven to 170°C fan. On a lightly floured surface, roll the dough to 3–4 mm thick. Cut into shapes with a cutter and arrange on a lined baking tray. Bake for 12–14 minutes, until the biscuits are just firm at the edges and set across the surface. They harden considerably as they cool — take them out before they look done. Leave on the tray for 5 minutes, then transfer to a rack.
